featured.name: EverMind EverMemOS
featured.tagline: Persistent memory infrastructure for AI agents. Plus an $80k dev competition.
featured.why_radar: Memory is the unsolved problem in agent stacks. EverMind tackles it with a dedicated memory OS for agents. They are backing it with an $80k developer competition running until April 4th, looking for plugins (VSCode, Chrome, Slack) and core optimisations. Serious money behind a real gap.

featured.name: AgentWeb
featured.tagline: Free business directory API for AI agents. 11M+ businesses across 195 countries.
featured.why_radar: Clean data layer for AI agents that need real-world business info. Phone numbers, emails, hours, addresses. No more scraping Google or parsing random websites. Full-text and geo search included. Free tier is generous.

picks.name: Mission Control
picks.tagline: Open-source task management built for AI coding agents.
picks.why_radar: Task board designed for working with Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf. Gives you visibility into what agents are doing, role-specific instructions, and clear work items. If you are running multiple agents on a codebase, this helps keep track.

hn_top5.title: Don't trust AI agents
hn_top5.summary: AI agents are fundamentally untrustworthy and pose serious security risks that developers are ignoring. The author argues for a zero-trust security model when dealing with AI systems, emphasising that current safeguards are inadequate. This is a necessary wake-up call for an industry rushing headlong into AI deployment without proper security considerations.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Now I Get It – Translate scientific papers into interactive webpages
hn_top5.summary: This tool converts dense scientific papers into interactive, digestible web pages that actually make sense to normal humans. It's a brilliant solution to the problem of academic papers being deliberately obscure and inaccessible. Finally, someone is using technology to democratise scientific knowledge instead of making it more complicated.

hn_top5.title: Garbage In, Garbage Out: The Degradation of Human Requirements in the LLM Era
hn_top5.summary: The rise of LLMs is making people lazy about defining clear requirements, leading to worse software outcomes. When humans stop thinking critically about what they actually want, they get rubbish results from AI systems. This is a predictable consequence of over-relying on tools that seem magical but still require human intelligence to use properly.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Mowgli – Figma for the agent era, with Claude Code and design export
hn_top5.summary: Another design tool trying to ride the AI wave by integrating Claude for code generation and design export. The 'Figma for agents' positioning feels like buzzword bingo rather than solving a real problem. The low engagement suggests the market isn't particularly excited about yet another AI-powered design platform.

hn_top5.title: Anthropic vs. DoD: "Any lawful use" is a fight about control
hn_top5.summary: Anthropic's partnership with the Department of Defence is sparking debate about AI ethics and corporate responsibility. The 'any lawful use' clause is concerning because it essentially gives military applications a blank cheque. This highlights the growing tension between AI companies' stated values and their commercial interests.
